question_text: Two identical boxes are pushed across different surfaces at the same speed: Box A across sandpaper, Box B across a polished marble floor. The same 30 N force is applied to each box. Explain why the boxes will have different accelerations, and predict which will accelerate more. (Ignore air resistance.)
correct_answer: The sandpaper has a rougher surface, creating greater friction than the polished marble. Box B (on marble) experiences less friction, so the net force is greater and it accelerates more. Box A experiences more friction, reducing the net force and its acceleration.
explanation: The roughness of a surface is a key factor affecting friction—rougher surfaces create more friction than smooth ones. Since both boxes experience the same applied force but different friction forces, their net forces and accelerations will differ.
